description: Retrieval evaluation dataset for the NIKE FY2023 10-K (nke-10k-2023.pdf). Each question has ground-truth page numbers (0-indexed, matching the 'page' metadata set in extract_pdf.py) that were manually verified against the extracted PDF text. A retrieved chunk counts as relevant if its source page is in expected_pages.

questions.question: What were NIKE's total revenues in fiscal 2023 and how much did they grow?
questions.reference_answer: Record revenues of $51.2 billion, up 10% on a reported basis and 16% on a currency-neutral basis.

questions.question: How much revenue did NIKE Direct generate in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: NIKE Direct revenues grew 14% from $18.7 billion in fiscal 2022 to $21.3 billion in fiscal 2023.

questions.question: What was NIKE's gross margin in fiscal 2023 and why did it change?
questions.reference_answer: Gross margin decreased 250 basis points to 43.5%, driven primarily by higher product costs, higher markdowns and unfavorable foreign currency changes.

questions.question: Approximately how many employees did NIKE have as of May 31, 2023?
questions.reference_answer: Approximately 83,700 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees.

questions.question: Where is Converse headquartered and what trademarks does it sell under?
questions.reference_answer: Converse is headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts and sells under the Converse, Chuck Taylor, All Star, One Star, Star Chevron and Jack Purcell trademarks.

questions.question: Describe NIKE's world headquarters campus.
questions.reference_answer: The NIKE World Campus near Beaverton, Oregon is an approximately 400-acre site with over 40 buildings occupied by approximately 11,400 employees.

questions.question: How many significant distribution centers does NIKE have in the United States and where are they located?
questions.reference_answer: Eight significant distribution centers in the United States; five are in or near Memphis, Tennessee, plus Indianapolis, Indiana and Dayton, Tennessee.

questions.question: What was NIKE's effective tax rate in fiscal 2023 and why did it change?
questions.reference_answer: The effective tax rate was 18.2% for fiscal 2023 compared to 9.1% for fiscal 2022, primarily due to decreased benefits from stock-based compensation.

questions.question: How much were Converse revenues in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: Converse revenues were $2,427 million in fiscal 2023, up 3% on a reported basis.

questions.question: What share repurchase program did NIKE begin purchasing under in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: A new four-year, $18 billion share repurchase program authorized by the Board of Directors in June 2022.

questions.question: How did foreign currency exchange rates affect NIKE's reported revenues in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: Fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates negatively impacted reported revenues by approximately $2,859 million.

questions.question: What is demand creation expense and how much was it in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: Demand creation expense consists of advertising and promotion costs including endorsement contracts; it was $4,060 million in fiscal 2023, up 5%.

questions.question: What was NIKE's net income in fiscal 2023?
questions.reference_answer: Net income was $5,070 million in fiscal 2023, compared to $6,046 million in fiscal 2022.

questions.question: Who is NIKE's independent registered public accounting firm?
